title: vaccinium pedris
side_effects: Vaccinium pedris can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including diarrhea and stomach cramps, due to its high concentration of tannins. Overconsumption of the plant may also lead to allergic reactions, including skin rashes and itching, as well as respiratory problems like wheezing and shortness of breath. In severe cases, excessive use of Vaccinium pedris can cause liver and kidney damage, requiring immediate medical attention to prevent long-term complications. Additionally, its use during pregnancy or breastfeeding may result in birth defects or harm to the fetus or infant.
